content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

October 30, 1980

MR. Paul Richards
District Manager
Sierra Planning Organization
115-72 B Avenue
Auburn, CA  95603

A-80-10-055

Dear Mr. Richards:

     This is in reply to a request from Barbara Hallatz that
we advise your agency concerning your duties to maintain
Statements of Economic Interests filed pursuant to your
Conflict of Interest Code. The following advice is provided
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).1/

     The Political Reform Act provides that Statements of
Economic Interests filed under Conflict of Interest Codes
should be filed as follows:

. . .

     (1) Heads of agencies, members of boards or commis-
sions not under a department of state government, or
members of boards or commissions not under the juris-
diction of a local legislative body, one original with
the agency, which shall make and retain a copy and
forward the original to the code reviewing body, which
shall be the filing officer. . . .

     (K) Persons not mentioned above - one original
with the agency or with the code reviewing body, as
provided by the code reviewing body in the agency's
conflict of interest code.

Section 87500.

     In addition, Section 81009 of the Political Reform Act
provides that Statements of Economic Interests are public
records, that originals of them should be maintained for

_________________________
1/ All statutory references made are to the Government
Code unless otherwise noted.

87008
87009

Mr. Paul Richards
October 30, 1980
Page Two


seven years, and that copies must be retained for four years.
Consequently, copies of the statements filed by you, as
District Manager, and by the members of the governing body,
should be maintained by your agency for four years; if the
agency hires any consultants who are required to file State-
ments, the originals of those statements would stay at the
agency and you would have to maintain them for seven years.

Finally, both originals and copies of Statements are
public records and must be available for public inspection
and reproduction during regular business hours. See Section
11008. Therefore, any provisions of law outside the Political
Reform Act, per se, that apply to the maintenance or retention
of public records would also apply to these documents.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate
to contact me.

Sincerely,


Sarah Cameron
Deputy Chief for
Conflicts of Interest
Legal Division

STC:kp

embedding.qa_text: October 30, 1980 Mr. Paul Richards District Manager Sierra Planning Organization 115-72 B Avenue Auburn, CA 95603 Dear Mr. Richards: This is in reply to a request from Barbara Hallatz that we advise your agency concerning your duties to maintain Statements of Economic Interests filed pursuant to your Conflict of Interest Code. The following advice is provided pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).1/ The Political Reform Act provides that Statements of Economic Interests filed under Conflict of Interest Codes should be filed as follows: (j) Heads of agencies, members of boards or commissions not under a department of state government, or members of boards or commissions not under the jurisdiction of a local legislative body, one original with the agency, which shall make and retain a copy and forward the original to the code reviewing body, which shall be the filing officer. (k) Persons not mentioned above - one original with the agency or with the code reviewing body, as provided by the code reviewing body in the agency's conflict of interest code. Section 87500. In addition, Section 81009 of the Political Reform Act provides that Statements of Economic Interests are public records, that originals of them should be maintained for 1/ All statutory references made are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. Mr. Paul Richards October 30, 1980 Page Two seven years, and that copies must be retained for four years. Consequently, copies of the Statements filed by you, as District Manager, and by the members of the Governing Body, should be maintained by your agency for four years; if the agency hires any consultants who are required to file Statements, the originals of those Statements would stay at the agency and you would have to maintain them for seven years. Finally, both originals and copies of Statements are public records and must be available for public inspection and reproduction during regular business hours. See Section 81008. Therefore, any provisions of law outside the Political Reform Act, per se, that apply to the maintenance or retention of public records would also apply to these documents. If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, Sarah Cameron Deputy Chief for Conflicts of Interest Legal Division
